mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the sales trend is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 406748 as of one month before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, lowering expected earnings and risk appetite, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 8.1 as of one month before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while unemployment remains elevated, investors price continued policy accommodation, lowering discount rates and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.13 and UNRATE was 8.1 before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-below-0.25 and UNRATE-above-7.5 regime does not deliver higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than other regimes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and operating leverage pressure, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 96.8202 as of one month before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if negative-signal hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in housing starts signals improving household formation, construction activity, and durable-goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 746 as of two months before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST acceleration triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation with a near-zero policy rate lowers real short rates and supports nominal earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days in a financial-repression regime.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2.5 and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 230.102 and FEDFUNDS was 0.13 before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-growth and FEDFUNDS-below-0.25 interaction has a nonpositive average 5-day forward return or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth signals broad demand deceleration and lower aggregate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when quarterly output growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 13564.5 as of five months before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth-below-1 triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if average triggered return exceeds the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate signal self-sustaining labor income growth, increasing consumption expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change exceeds 125 and UNRATE falls over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 133300 and UNRATE was 8.1 before 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and unemployment-decline signal does not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than either single-series signal in walk-forward tests.
